metadata.ranking_source: QS Asia University Rankings 2026 (released November 2025) and QS World University Rankings 2026
metadata.ranking_notes: Philippine ranking = rank among PH universities in QS Asia 2026. Universities not appearing in QS are noted as 'Not ranked'. Many smaller/local/LGU-funded universities are not ranked in QS.
metadata.acceptance_rate_notes: Acceptance rates are estimates based on published data from EduRank, UniRank, and institutional sources. Public LGU-funded universities generally have open admission policies for residents.
metadata.tuition_notes: Tuition fees are approximate and subject to change each AY. Always verify with the university's official website. Tuition for public universities under RA 10931 (Universal Access to Quality Tertiary Education Act) is free for qualified students; miscellaneous fees still apply.

universities.name: University of the Philippines Manila (UPM)
universities.info: The country's premier institution for health sciences and medical education.
universities.ph_ranking: #1 in Philippines (UP System; QS Asia 2026 — 104th in Asia, #1 in PH)
universities.acceptance_rate: ~15% (very selective; uses UPCAT for most programs; MD uses separate NMAT)
programs: Doctor of Medicine (MD)
Doctor of Dental Medicine (DMD)
Bachelor of Science in Nursing (BSN)
Bachelor of Science in Pharmacy (BSPharm)
Bachelor of Science in Physical Therapy
Bachelor of Science in Occupational Therapy
Bachelor of Science in Medical Laboratory Science
Bachelor of Science in Radiologic Technology
Bachelor of Science in Nutrition and Dietetics
Bachelor of Arts in Social Sciences
Bachelor of Science in Public Health
Master of Public Health (MPH)
Doctor of Public Health (DrPH)

tuition.undergraduate: Free under RA 10931 for qualified undergraduates. Miscellaneous fees: approx. PHP 1,000-3,000 per semester. Unsubsidized tuition: approx. PHP 1,500/unit.
tuition.medicine_md: Additional clinical fees; approx. PHP 30,000-60,000/year above tuition coverage
tuition.graduate: PHP 2,500-4,500 per unit depending on program

universities.name: University of Santo Tomas (UST)
universities.info: Founded in 1611, it is the oldest university in Asia and a premier Catholic institution.
universities.acceptance_rate: ~81-90% (uses USTET entrance exam; ~44,000+ applications per cycle)
universities.total_estimated_students: ~44,812 (AY 2025-2026; official UST data; largest single-campus Catholic university in the world)
programs: Doctor of Medicine (MD)
Doctor of Dental Medicine (DMD) — not currently offered at main campus
Bachelor of Science in Nursing (BSN)
Bachelor of Science in Pharmacy (BSPharm)
Bachelor of Science in Physical Therapy
Bachelor of Science in Medical Laboratory Science
Bachelor of Science in Radiologic Technology
Bachelor of Science in Accountancy (BSA)
Bachelor of Science in Business Administration (BSBA)
Bachelor of Arts in Liberal Arts (various majors)
Bachelor of Arts in Communication
Bachelor of Science in Architecture
Bachelor of Science in Civil Engineering
Bachelor of Science in Electrical Engineering
Bachelor of Science in Chemical Engineering
Bachelor of Science in Mechanical Engineering
Bachelor of Science in Industrial Engineering
Bachelor of Fine Arts
Bachelor of Science in Psychology
Bachelor of Science in Tourism Management
Bachelor of Secondary Education (BSEd)
Juris Doctor (JD / Law)
Master of Business Administration (MBA)
Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) in various disciplines
Master of Arts / Master of Science in various disciplines (50+ programs)

tuition.note: See https://www.ust.edu.ph/tuition-fees/ for exact per-program breakdown per AY

tuition.undergraduate: Free under RA 10931 for qualified students (Socialized Tuition System). Without subsidy: approx. PHP 1,500/unit (21-unit load = ~PHP 31,500 + misc fees ~PHP 37,243 total per semester).
tuition.graduate: PHP 2,500 per unit (standard); PHP 2,000/unit for government employees
tuition.note: UPD uses the Socialized Tuition and Financial Assistance Program (STFAP); most Filipino students pay minimal to zero tuition.
